name: Saag Gosht (Beef in Fragrant Spinach Sauce)
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds boneless beef round or lamb, cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces
3 tablespoons fresh ginger root, finely chopped
1 cinnamon stick, broken into small pieces
2 tablespoons light vegetable oil, if needed
recipeInstructions:
Separately cook the spinach and fold it into the meat just before serving to ensure it holds its color and flavor.
Preheat oven to 325°F.
Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large frying pan over high heat. Brown beef pieces, turning them frequently until nicely seared (best done in batches). Transfer browned pieces to a heavy-bottomed casserole.
Add remaining oil, onions, and fry until caramel brown (about 25 minutes), stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add garlic and ginger; fry for an additional 2 minutes. Add cumin, tomatoes, and chiles; continue frying until mixture is thick and pulpy (about 3 minutes). Turn off heat, then puree in a blender or food processor and add to the meat.
Wrap cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, and bay leaves in cheesecloth, tie tightly, and crush spices. Add spice bag to casserole.
Add 4 cups boiling water and salt; stir to distribute meat in sauce. Cover pot and place on stove, then return to oven for 2 1/2 hours (or cook over low heat for 2-2.5 hours until tender). Remove lid, take off spice bag, squeeze out juice, discard bag.
Add cooked spinach and garam masala; blend well without breaking meat pieces. Cover pot and return to oven or stove, cooking an additional 5 minutes. Turn off oven, let rest 10 minutes; adjust salt if needed, stirring in a few tablespoons oil for glaze.
Serve with Pilaf or plain rice.
Note: This dish tastes better after resting; it's best made ahead of time and reheated gently before serving. It stores well refrigerated up to 2 days and freezes well. Thaw thoroughly before reheating by simmering over low heat until warmed through, then adjust salt and fold in a little garam masala before serving.
